title: microschizaea hallieri
intro: Microschizaea hallieri, also known as the Hall's fork fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Lindsaeaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Microschizaea hallieri can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
